threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You don’t know the way to navigate around a roundabout without looking lost—every local has a secret route they've mastered.
Caught a guy on the 36 last night singing opera at the back. Had the whole bus swinging in their seats, weirdly uplifting for a Tuesday.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I heard a busker playing the kora on Malchower Straße while a crowd gathered, I knew this place had a pulse unlike anywhere else.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain do they get in-depth discussions about the city’s bus routes over a beer at the corner kiosk—true local tradition.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from the Mission when they can name more than two taco spots on 24th without missing a beat. The newbies stick to the tourist traps.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Spotted a guy juggling on the 19 Polk today while the bus came to a full stop. Just your typical Tenderloin commute, folks.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all locals hit the beach every weekend, but many of us prefer a quiet afternoon in the Plaça del Sol with a book and a vermut.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still struggling to find a café that serves a proper cortado and asking for ‘milk coffee’ at 7pm.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still say 'the Tube' instead of 'the Overground' like it's not a complete lifestyle change. That's an instant giveaway.
Caught a night bus from Brixton to Clapham and overheard a guy talking about his cat named 'Mr. Snuggles' – he said he’s preparing for his first birthday party. Brixton’s full of surprises!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I found a fresh corner store selling samosas at 2 AM, I realized this place never sleeps. That's the Neukölln spirit, isn't it?

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Kreuzberg, you can find a café where they serve coffee with a side of political pamphlets. It’s like sipping activism with your espresso.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone's from here if they smell the BART equipment problem from a block away and still decide to take it anyway. That's true grit.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy in a tuxedo singing to tourists at the stop near Union Square. Muni’s never boring; you never know when it turns into a live show.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Most tourists think Gràcia is all about Gaudí, but the real charm lies in the little corner bars where locals argue about Barça over cheap vermut.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell them by their enthusiasm for making eye contact and asking for directions, even when they’re already on the right path.
